lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Greetings traveller. Welcome to 'The Hair Of The Dog' Tavern. What can I do you for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a beer?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Well that's my speciality! The local brew's named 'Moonlight Mead' and will set you back 5 gold. Whaddya say? Fancy a pint?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have the money on me right now though... can I start a tab?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: You see that sign behind me there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that says; 'Please Do Not Ask For Credit As Being Attacked By A Large Angry Werewolf Inn Keeper Often Offends'?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Bingo.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Here ya go pal. Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Eh, suit yourself. You're missing out on a genuine taste experience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I hear some gossip?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Well, I dunno... The village is kind of on the fringe out here, I dunno how up to date the stuff I hear about is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this village.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: You want to know about Canifis? I dunno why, not a lot happens here. We're just your typical everyday down at earth werewolf folk, after all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... everyone here is a werewolf?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Yep. We are as Zamorak made us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mentioned Zamorak...

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Yeah, he's great isn't he? Every year we hold a big festival to give thanks to Zamorak for keeping us well fed and happy here.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: I hear over in the West they worship Saradomin, but I dunno why! What's he ever done to help out us werewolves, huh?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: I hear he has all these crazy followers who say things like we shouldn't kill people and eat them! What's up with that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...So when is this festival?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Aaaah, not for a good few months yet. Come and ask me again nearer to the time, I'll keep some extra meat and mead back from the rest for ya pal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the land of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Well, I don't know what to tell you really... This village is called Canifis and lies on the border between Morytania and Misthalin... so we're kind of on the front line if those Saradominists to the west ever decide to

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: attack us... South East of here is the castle of Lord Drakan, our master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Drakan? Who is that?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Ahhh... you must be new to these parts if you haven't heard of Lord Drakan! He's the lord of this land, and we all pledge allegiance to him.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: In return for our allegiance, and the tithe of course, he keeps our land safe from any invaders and the Saradominists who want to kill us all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tithe? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Ah, well, in return for his protection, we have to give Lord Drakan a share of blood every week. If we don't have any to spare from our hunts, then we need to pick a member of the village to give their life in return

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: for the blood so that the tithe is fulfilled.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you kill someone you know in order to meet the tithe???

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: That's right, but only if we haven't managed to get enough spare blood from our hunts. It's kind of severe if you look at it that way, but frankly, I think the price is fair in return for his protection and his tolerance of

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: our village. He could probably kill us all if he wanted us gone, so keeping on his good side is worth the sacrifice to us. Lucky we're not human really!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: He hates humans! Apparently long ago his brother Draynor was trapped in Misthalin and lost much of his powers, and was recently killed by some human!

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: He hates humans! Apparently long ago his brother Draynor was trapped in Misthalin, and adventurous little human wannabe-heroes keep trying to kill him!

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Man, I'd hate to be a human around here, that's for sure. Drakan would really enjoy hunting them down and killing them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... thanks for the info...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the shopkeepers here.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Hmmm? Why, who did you want to hear the gossip about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me what you know about Sbott the Tanner.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Hey, I won't hear a word said bad about that guy! He's an honest and hard-working wolf if I ever met one! He charges a lot for his job, but he's one of the best tanners I've ever seen - and I'm over four hundred

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: years old! You need stuff tanning, I recommend him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know anything interesting about Rufus the food seller?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Ah yeah... good old Rufus... He's kind of getting on in years and doesn't like to come out of his wolf form so often, but let me tell you this: that guy is a hunter through and through. You seen how much food he

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: catches a day? That takes real dedication! Some of the young pups think just because a wolf has a bit of grey in his fur then he's past it, but I've seen him put those pups to shame in a hunt! He's a real inspiration to us

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got anything to share about Barker the clothes seller?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Eh, I don't like the guy much, but you can't knock the quality of his stock. They're some fine quality threads you can get yourself there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong with him?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Eh... I can't really tell you to be honest. Something about the guy just gets my hackles up everytime he opens his yap, know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Lucky for you. I can't really explain it, I just don't like the guy. Does great clothes though, you got to give him that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet you have some juicy gossip about Fidelio the general store owner.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: That nut job? Oh sure, we all know about him. He was a real firebrand daredevil when he was younger, always taking risks to make himself a quick buck... he got himself caught up in the smuggling trade, sneaking over

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: the border into Misthalin and pretending to be a human so he could buy exotic items to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exotic items? From Misthalin? Like what?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Oh man, you name it! Flour... buckets... tinderboxes... chisels... he could get the lot! He's been doing it so long now though, his nerves are shot.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Nowadays he's a disgrace, he can barely string a sentence together and jumps at his own shadow. Makes you feel ashamed to be a werewolf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the temple to the west.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Well, I'm not old enough to remember the full story behind it, but it was a terrible day for our kingdom when it was built there.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Apparently Morytania had a once strong kingdom, with lands spreading far further west than they do today, and south into the desert, until the day when a sneak attack by the hated human forces who worship

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Saradomin burned our villages and slaughtered our peoples in mass.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: They then cursed the river, so that it would burn our kind should we touch it! Can you imagine??? To make an entire river poisonous to us???

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Luckily they seem to have calmed down somewhat recently, and tend to stay on their side of the river... but the wrong they have done my people will never be forgotten, and will never be forgiven. What is worse is

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: that they then had the nerve to build that gigantic statue on the river mouth to mock the slaughter of our people and the poisoning of our river! I can understand Lord Drakan's hatred for them even if I do not share

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: it to his extent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you hate humans too?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Absolutely! If I ever met one I would gobble him up where he stands! And then chew the bones for dessert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Um, okay, thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I hear a story?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: A story??? Heh, well the only one I can think of right now is one my dear old mammy told me as a pup...

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Now how did it go... Ah yes!

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Once upon a time a brave young wolf was walking through a forest, when he came upon a human dressed all in red. 'Aha!' he thought to himself, 'Here's a nice easy meal!' But the human talked to him, and as he was

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: always taught to be a polite wolf, he spoke back to it.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Well, this cunning human told him that there was a better meal that would not run away in a house in the woods. As the brave young wolf could see that this human was not fully grown, he figured maybe he'd

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: better get a better meal at this house, so he ran as fast as his paws could carry him to the house the human told him about... inside he found an old human lying in bed, and although the meat was a little tough, as the

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: human was older than he thought, he had a good meal and decided to sleep it off in the house for awhile. He had not been asleep long though, when he was woken by a knocking at the door. The human in red had followed

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: him to the house! Suspecting a human trap, the brave young wolf put on the old humans clothes and jumped into the bed, so that he could pretend to be human and escape from this terrible trap! Well now, this human

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: dressed in red came into the house, and pretended to believe the brave wolf was really a human, and began to talk to him. But then the human started asking strange questions of the wolf, because the human knew that it

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: was not a human at all! The brave young wolf decided to try and escape, for it was only a young human, and not very strong, so the brave young wolf said he would eat the human if they did not let him escape! As he said

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: this however, the human in red shouted out at him: 'Aha! You are a wolf!' and as the human shouted this another bigger human ran into the room from outside! This bigger human was much stronger and was

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: carrying an axe, and the poor young wolf died in this terrible trap made for him by the humans.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: And do you know what the moral of this story is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... no, not really.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: It's 'Never trust humans' of course! My dear old mammy told me that story when I was a pup, and I'm still alive and well to tell it to you today!

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Pretty good story huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... yeah. It was great. Really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: ...I don't know why you talked to me if you don't want anything then...

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Here you go!
